prose:
Method–provenance summary for the computed frontier-orbital gap of water:

• Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) energy gap, units eV (positive value = |εLUMO – εHOMO|).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’  ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF.
• Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied geometry in /Users/evane/…/water.xyz; no further optimisation (notation: DFT//user xyz).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvent).
• Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: PySCF (invoked via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill; see CLI in JSON).  SCF converged in 7 cycles.

Result
HOMO = –8.6037183 eV
LUMO =  0.33695037 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV  (reported above under value)

Warnings: none.

Reproducibility
Live engine log was written during the run (frontier_<timestamp>.out in the calling directory); result JSON at /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmpzxglfa16/live.json captures the full CLI invocation and all raw outputs.
